m_Item1.QuestionText: What does MVC in Spring MVC stands for?
m_Item1.Explanation: MVC stands for Model View Controller

m_Item1.QuestionText: The front controller that intercepts the incoming request in spring mvc is known as _______________
m_Item1.Explanation: DispatcherServlet intercepts incomming request in spring mvc.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is Bean in Spring?
m_Item1.Explanation: Bean is an object in spring.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which base interface in spring is responsible for instantiating and managing the so called Spring beans?
m_Item1.Explanation: BeanFactory is base interface responsible for Instantiating and managing the so called Spring beans. It has various implementing classes. ApplicationContext is designed on top of the BeanFactory interface

m_Item1.QuestionText: How many types of lOC (dependency injection)?
m_Item1.Explanation: Setter Injection,Constructor Injection,Interface Injection are types of IOC.

m_Item1.QuestionText: @Component annotation on class indicates

Options.OptionText: that a bean should be created for the class
that a bean should not be created for the class
that autowiring should be enabled for the class
that autowiring should not be enabled for the class

m_Item1.Explanation: A bean should be created for the class

m_Item1.QuestionText: DAO methods require access to the session factory, which can be injected:-
m_Item1.Explanation: To use the contextual session approach, your DAO methods require access to the session factory, which can be injected via a setter method or a constructor argument.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Annotation to find a transaction and then fail, complaining that no Hibernate session been bound to the thread.
m_Item1.Explanation: This ensures that the persistence operations within a DAO method will be executed in the same

m_Item1.QuestionText: Annotation for Hibernate exceptions to be translated into Spring DataAccessException for consistent exception handling:-
m_Item1.Explanation: If you want the Hibernate exceptions to be translated into Spring DataAccessException for consistent exception handling, you have to apply the @Repository annotation to your DAO class that requires exception translation.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following could act as a possible view in spring mvc?
m_Item1.Explanation: html and jsp are possible views in spring mvc.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Spring MVC can define how many controller(s)?
m_Item1.Explanation: In Spring MVC we can define multiple controllers.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is provided by Spring Boot?

Options.OptionText: Support for Jetty and Undertow as embedded containers
Auto-configuration of the Spring Framework and third libraries
Convenient dependency descriptors to load transitive dependencies

m_Item1.Explanation: All options are correct.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the name of the default environment configuration file of Spring Boot?
m_Item1.Explanation: application.propertiesis the name of the default environment configuration file of spring boot.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the right location for application.properties file?
m_Item1.Explanation: resources folder is used to store all your project resources files like , xml files, images, text files and etc

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is  the advantage of spring boot?

Options.OptionText: Embed Tomcat, Jetty or Undertow directly. You don't need to deploy WAR files.
It provides opinionated 'starter' POMs to simplify your Maven configuration.
It automatically configure Spring whenever possible.

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring boot framework has embedded tmcat server, Maven configuration is easy by using starters and configures automatically wherever possible.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of these is not true of Spring Boot?

Options.OptionText: One can build more with less code
It can be run straight from a command line

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot is non-invasive because which never forces the programmer to inherits any class or implements any interface from spring framework. So it is called non-invasive.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not true for Spring Boot framework?
m_Item1.Explanation:
For Spring Boot,
 No external web server
 No need of XML configuration
 No need of WAR files
 but we need a starter POM files.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which among these is not an applications server provided by Spring Boot?
m_Item1.Explanation: Binary link is not an applications server provided by spring boot.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which is a peculiar quality of the Spring Boot platform?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot is micro-service ready.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which out of the following properties can be defined in application.properties file in spring boot application which exists in a classpath of the application?
m_Item1.Explanation: application.properties file contains database details, server port and view information etc.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which out of the following are correct as key points offered by spring boot but not by spring
a. Starter POM.
b. Jar files Version Management.
c. Auto Configuration.
d. Component Scanning.
e. Embedded server.
f.  InMemory DB configuration.
m_Item1.Explanation: All are the features made available in spring boot to create production ready applications in lesser time except component scanning. It was available even in spring

m_Item1.QuestionText: The entry point of the spring boot application is the class that contains _____________ annotation and the main method.
m_Item1.Explanation: Execution begins from main method of a class which is annotated with @SpringBootApplication

m_Item1.QuestionText: What amongst following is/are the advatage(s) of spring starter dependencies?

Options.OptionText: Starter dependencies speed up development and reduce the number of dependencies required for an application
Developers do not need to know the names of the dependencies required for an application
Developers do not need to know the exact version of the dependencies to use

m_Item1.Explanation: --

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary feature of Spring Boot?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot simplifies the development of new Spring applications through convention over configuration and automatic setup.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the @SpringBootApplication annotation do?
m_Item1.Explanation: The annotation is a convenience for configuring a Spring application that declares one or more @Bean methods and triggers auto-configuration.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which module of Spring Framework is the foundation for Spring Boot?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Core provides the fundamental parts of the framework, such as dependency injection, which are essential for Spring Boot.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What role does Spring Boot DevTools play?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot DevTools provides features like automatic restart and live reload to enhance developer productivity.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What annotation is used to define a Spring Boot application’s main class?
m_Item1.Explanation: The @SpringBootApplication annotation is used to mark a configuration class that declares one or more @Bean methods and triggers auto-configuration and component scanning.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The @SpringBootApplication annotation in Spring Boot is equivalent to which combination of annotations?
m_Item1.Explanation: The @SpringBootApplication annotation is a convenience annotation that combines three other annotations: @Configuration, @EnableAutoConfiguration, and @ComponentScan. This combination allows developers to define Spring beans, enable Spring Boot's auto-configuration, and specify the packages to scan for components with just one annotation.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the use of the @SpringBootApplication annotation?

Options.OptionText: To enable batch processing in a Spring application.
To execute a main method in a Spring application.
To denote a configuration class that declares one or more @Bean methods.
It is a convenience annotation that adds @Configuration, @EnableAutoConfiguration, and @ComponentScan.

m_Item1.Explanation: @SpringBootApplication includes @Configuration, @EnableAutoConfiguration, and @ComponentScan, simplifying config.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary purpose of the @SpringBootApplication annotation in a Spring Boot application?

Options.OptionText: To scan for beans in the package
To enable auto-configuration, component scan, and configuration
To deploy the application on the server

m_Item1.Explanation: The @SpringBootApplication annotation is a convenience annotation that combines @Configuration, @EnableAutoConfiguration, and @ComponentScan.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a feature provided by Spring Boot?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot provides features like embedded server, convention over configuration, and production-ready metrics, but it does not directly handle AJAX integration.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which Spring Boot starter would you include to build a web application with RESTful services?
m_Item1.Explanation: The spring-boot-starter-web starter includes all the dependencies to create a RESTful web application using Spring MVC.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does Spring Boot facilitate application configuration in a consistent manner?

Options.OptionText: By externalizing configuration using properties or YAML files
By hardcoding configuration settings in Java classes

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot supports externalized configuration, allowing developers to use .properties and .yml files to manage application settings in a consistent manner across environments.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which command is used to run a Spring Boot application from the command line?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'java -jar' command is used to start a Spring Boot application from the command line when you have packaged your application into a JAR file.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a Spring Boot application, what is the default embedded server?
m_Item1.Explanation: By default, Spring Boot ships with embedded Tomcat as the default web server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the role of the application.properties file in a Spring Boot project?
m_Item1.Explanation: The application.properties file is used to define environment-specific settings such as server port, database configuration, and more.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is Spring Boot Actuator used for?

Options.OptionText: To bootstrap the application on cloud
To monitor and manage the application in production environment

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot Actuator provides endpoints for monitoring and managing a Spring Boot application, including metrics, health checks, heap dump, and more.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you exclude auto-configuration classes from your Spring Boot application?
m_Item1.Explanation: You can use @SpringBootApplication(exclude={ClassName.class}) to exclude specific auto-configuration classes from a Spring Boot application.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which file format is not directly supported for external configurations in Spring Boot?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot supports configuration via .properties and .yml (YAML) files, but JSON is not supported directly for configuration files.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of Spring Boot DevTools?

Options.OptionText: To support development-time features, such as automatic restarts and remote debugging

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot DevTools enhances the development experience by providing features such as automatic restarts, live reload, and remote debugging.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which property can be used to change the default server port in a Spring Boot application?
m_Item1.Explanation: The server.port property is used in the application.properties or application.yml file to change the default port (8080) to a custom port.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do you enable asynchronous processing in a Spring Boot application?
m_Item1.Explanation: @EnableAsync is used to enable asynchronous method execution capabilities in a Spring Boot application.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which exception is thrown when a Bean cannot be found in the Spring context?
m_Item1.Explanation: NoSuchBeanDefinitionException is thrown when Spring cannot find a bean definition in the ApplicationContext that matches the requirements.

m_Item1.QuestionText: When running a Spring Boot application, what does the ApplicationContextInitializer interface allow you to do?

Options.OptionText: Customize the ApplicationContext before it is refreshed

m_Item1.Explanation: The ApplicationContextInitializer interface is a callback mechanism to customize ConfigurableApplicationContext before it is refreshed.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which annotation can be used to inject bean properties at runtime from the application properties?
m_Item1.Explanation: The @Value annotation is used to inject values into a Spring bean from application properties or environment variables.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What mechanism does Spring Boot offer for handling application startup failure exceptions?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot uses Failure Analyzers to provide diagnostics and actionable messages for startup failures in a Spring Boot application context.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In a Spring Boot MVC application, which annotation is used to mark a class as a controller?
m_Item1.Explanation: In a Spring Boot MVC application, the @RestController annotation is used to mark a class as a controller that handles requests and provides RESTful web services. It is a specialized version of @Component class annotation.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which annotation is used to map a method in a Controller class to a specific HTTP GET request?
m_Item1.Explanation: @GetMapping is the annotation used in Spring Boot MVC to map HTTP GET requests onto specific handler methods.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In Spring Boot MVC, what is the purpose of the @ModelAttribute annotation?

Options.OptionText: To bind HTTP request data to method parameters.

m_Item1.Explanation: The @ModelAttribute annotation is used to bind HTTP request data to method parameters, allowing data from a form to be bound to a Model object easily.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the default behavior of Spring Boot for handling static resources?

Options.OptionText: Does not serve static resources by default

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot, by default, serves static resources from locations such as /static, /public, /resources, and /META-INF/resources.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you execute custom logic before a controller returns a view name in Spring Boot MVC?
m_Item1.Explanation: A HandlerInterceptor can be used to execute custom logic before the execution of a request handler (e.g., a controller method) and before the view is rendered.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which property file does Spring Boot use by default to configure the application?
m_Item1.Explanation: By default, Spring Boot loads configuration properties from application.properties located in the src/main/resources directory.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method is used in Spring Boot MVC to add attributes to the model?
m_Item1.Explanation: The addAttribute() method is used to add a key-value pair to the model, allowing data to be passed to the view.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a disadvantage of using Spring Boot, a Spring-based framework, for building large enterprise systems?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot's auto-configuration capabilities, which simplify development, can lead to increased resource consumption due to the inclusion of more components and dependencies by default.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which advantage does Spring Boot bring to Spring Framework development?

Options.OptionText: It requires manual configuration for each dependency.
It simplifies the deployment of web apps with its embedded server feature.
It mandates the use of only Maven for build automation tasks.
It discourages the use of microservices architecture.

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot facilitates rapid development, especially for web-based applications, by providing features like an embedded web server, which eliminates container deployment.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is NOT a commonly cited advantage of using Spring Boot over the traditional Spring Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot simplifies configuration by using annotations and properties files instead of XML. A key advantage is less reliance on cumbersome XML configuration.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary advantage of Spring Boot's opinionated defaults?

Options.OptionText: They require detailed customization for every application.
They allow rapid development without setup overhead.
They are always mandatory and cannot be altered.
They support only traditional monolithic applications.

m_Item1.Explanation: Opinionated defaults in Spring Boot streamline the development process by providing ready-to-use configurations that prevent boilerplate code and reduce setup time.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which feature of Spring Boot improves development productivity compared to Spring Framework?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot enhances development productivity with integrated dependency management using Maven or Gradle, which automates the inclusion of necessary libraries.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does Spring Boot’s starter dependency approach benefit developers?

Options.OptionText: By requiring manual configuration of each dependency
By providing a convenient set of dependencies to simplify setup

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot starters provide a curated set of dependencies, which simplifies the setup process and ensures compatibility between components, facilitating quicker project initialization.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What role does the embedded server play in Spring Boot applications?

Options.OptionText: It makes deploying applications directly to a server more complex.
It eliminates the need for a separate application server during development.
It requires additional operational overhead to configure.

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot applications include an embedded server, which allows developers to run applications independently from a traditional application server, thus simplifying deployment and testing.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which advantage does Spring Boot's built-in server monitoring tool provide?

Options.OptionText: It supports only basic application logging.
It requires third-party integrations for any monitoring capabilities.
It offers out-of-the-box application metrics and insights.
It is limited to monitoring network traffic only.

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot includes an Actuator tool that offers comprehensive application metrics and insights, thus enabling developers to monitor and manage applications effectively without additional setups.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does Spring Boot simplify application testing compared to the traditional Spring framework?

Options.OptionText: By eliminating the need for testing entirely
By providing tools and annotations for easier context setup
By increasing the complexity of writing test cases
By mandating the use of an external test framework

m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot enhances testing by providing pre-configured testing annotations and tools, such as @SpringBootTest, which streamline context setup and facilitate comprehensive testing.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What annotation in Spring Boot MVC is used to map HTTP requests to handler methods of MVC controllers?
m_Item1.Explanation: @RequestMapping is used to map HTTP requests to specific handler methods of MVC controllers, allowing you to define URLs and HTTP methods.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In Spring Boot MVC, which feature assists in validating request body parameters automatically?
m_Item1.Explanation: Spring Boot MVC uses the Validation API, powered by annotations like @Valid, to automatically validate request body parameters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following annotations is used in Spring Boot MVC for form data validation?
m_Item1.Explanation: The @Valid annotation in Spring Boot MVC is used to indicate that a form-backing object should be validated before passing it to a controller method.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the purpose of the @ResponseBody annotation in Spring Boot MVC?

Options.OptionText: Automatically serialize the response object to JSON
Indicate the presence of a service class

m_Item1.Explanation: @ResponseBody is used to make a controller return the value (usually in JSON format) of methods, instead of a view reference.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In Spring Boot MVC, what role does the @Controller annotation serve?

Options.OptionText: It indicates configuration settings for the application
It marks a class as a Spring MVC controller

m_Item1.Explanation: @Controller is used to mark a Java class as a Spring MVC controller, allowing it to handle HTTP requests.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is true about Spring Boot MVC’s ModelAndView?

Options.OptionText: It is used to establish database connections
It is used to hold both the model and the view
It is used exclusively for REST APIs

m_Item1.Explanation: ModelAndView is used to encapsulate both the model attributes and the view name in a single return value, typically for rendering traditional MVC views.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How does @PathVariable support RESTful web services in Spring Boot MVC?
m_Item1.Explanation: @PathVariable annotation in Spring Boot MVC extracts values from the URL dynamically, supporting RESTful services by allowing placeholders in the URI.
